title: determine_provider: stringly-typed if-else dispatch with brittle prefix matching
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Dimension: Functional Correctness / Semantic

Location: src/proxy.rs lines 1148-1174 (fn determine_provider).

Violation: Provider dispatch is a stringly-typed `if/else-if` chain over model-name prefix matching. This is:
  (a) OCP violation — adding a new provider (e.g., `mistral`, `cohere`, `xai` for Grok) requires editing the function. It is not open for extension.
  (b) Brittle prefix matching — `starts_with("o1")` will match any future model named "o100", "observer", etc. `starts_with("claude")` will match a hypothetical `claude-lab-experimental` from a third party running on an OpenAI-compatible server.
  (c) Architectural coupling — returns `String` (allocation every call) despite the comment 'Returns a static string — no allocation needed' (line 1146) — the early-return branch returns `config.proxy.target.clone()` (line 1171) which IS an allocation, and the tail `provider.to_string()` (line 1173) also allocates.
  (d) The function silently shadows a user's proxy.target config when the model name is parseable. If a user sets proxy.target=openai but types `claude-3-sonnet`, we route to anthropic — not what the user configured.

Mandated Refactor:
  1. Define `pub enum Provider { Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Zai, DeepSeek, Qwen, Ollama, Custom(String) }` in src/providers/mod.rs.
  2. Add a static routing table: `static MODEL_PREFIX_ROUTES: &[(&str, Provider)]` populated from each adapter module via a `pub const MODEL_PREFIXES: &[&str]` declaration on each adapter.
  3. determine_provider returns `Provider` (copy-able enum), not String. Callers that need a name call `.name() -> &'static str`.
  4. Honor proxy.target as an EXPLICIT override: if target is set to a specific provider (not "auto"), route there regardless of model prefix. Only fall back to prefix-matching when target is "auto".
  5. Use word-boundary matching: `"o1" | "o1-preview" | "o3" | "o3-mini" | "o4-mini"` exact list, NOT prefix. Keep the list next to the OpenAI adapter definition.
